message:
fix(strict_git): close git global-flags bypass on every pattern

Git's global flags (`-C <path>`, `-c <key>=<value>`, `--git-dir=`,
`--work-tree=`, `--exec-path=`) go BEFORE the subcommand. Every
pattern in strict_git.rs that used `git\s+<sub>` failed to match
these mainline shapes:

  git -C /path/to/repo push origin --force
  git -c user.email=bot@corp.com rebase -i HEAD~3
  git --git-dir=/prod/.git commit --amend
  git -C /repo filter-branch --tree-filter 'rm -f secret' HEAD
  git -C ./submodule worktree remove dead
  git -C /prod add .
  git -C /prod add -A

`-C` in particular is routine on CI servers and any shell script
that operates on multiple worktrees from a parent directory.

Apply `git\b.*?\b<sub>` to the 11 destructive patterns that used
the `git\s+<sub>` shape: push --force, rebase, commit --amend,
cherry-pick, filter-branch, filter-repo, reflog expire, gc
--aggressive/--prune, worktree remove, submodule deinit, add .,
add -A / --all.

The two `push-master` / `push-main` patterns already have an
explicit `(?:\S+\s+)*` in them to handle flags; those are left
alone.

Regression test `strict_git_patterns_match_with_git_global_flags`
covers the seven highest-value shapes (push --force, rebase,
commit --amend, filter-branch, worktree remove, add ., add -A).

Full suite: 2,152 passed, 0 failed.
